name: Frank, Frank, Goldstein, & Nager PC
description_short: New York debt collection law firm specializing in litigation, judgment enforcement, and debt defense for both creditors and debtors. AV Preeminent-rated with 80+ years combined experience.
description_long:
Frank, Frank, Goldstein & Nager PC is a specialized debt collection law firm based in New York that has focused exclusively on debt-related legal matters for over 80 years combined. The firm was recognized as one of Inc. 5000's Fastest-Growing Companies and holds an AV Preeminent rating from Martindale-Hubbell, indicating high professional standards.

The firm operates on both sides of debt disputes. For creditors, they offer demand letters, arbitration, mediation, litigation, and judgment enforcement services including domestication of foreign judgments and collection of 20-year New York State judgments. For consumers and businesses being pursued for payment, they provide debt defense representation, help vacating default judgments, assistance unfreezing restrained bank accounts, and defense against merchant cash advance defaults and personal guarantee enforcement.

What distinguishes FFGN is their consultative, industry-specific approach rather than one-size-fits-all collection tactics. They explicitly tailor strategy based on underlying agreements and regulatory frameworks governing specific industries. The firm offers flexible fee arrangements (flat fee, contingency, and hybrid options) and emphasizes transparent communication throughout the collection or defense process. President Jocelyn Nager has been with the firm for over 25 years.

However, the firm's website contains some red flags: multiple Russian casino links (пинап казино, 1win зеркало) embedded in the site content, suggesting either poor website security or questionable practices. While the firm legitimately serves debtors defending against collections, their primary marketing emphasizes creditor recovery and ROI optimization. Consumers should recognize this is fundamentally a debt collection firm, though they do genuinely offer debt defense services.
address: 330 W 38th St Suite 701, New York, NY 10018

company_info.headquarters: 330 W 38th St Suite 701, New York, NY 10018

pros: AV Preeminent rating from Martindale-Hubbell indicates verified legal credentials and ethics
Dual-sided practice means they understand debt defense deeply, not just creditor collection
Flexible fee arrangements (flat fee, contingency, hybrid) rather than fixed percentage-only models
Specialized expertise in 20-year New York State judgment enforcement and domestication
Explicit focus on industry-specific representation with customized strategies
Free consultation available for judgment enforcement and default judgment vacation
PCI compliant operations ensure secure handling of financial data
cons: Website contains embedded Russian casino links suggesting security compromise or ethical concerns
Firm's primary business model is creditor-side collection, creating potential conflict-of-interest optics for debt defense clients
Limited geographic scope—explicitly focused on New York jurisdiction only
No published fee schedule or pricing transparency on website; requires consultation for quotes
No mention of FDCPA compliance guarantees or consumer protection certifications despite debt defense offerings
best_for: New York business creditors seeking specialized collection services with industry-specific expertise
New York consumers and businesses defending against debt collection lawsuits or wage garnishment
Debtors with existing judgments seeking to vacate defaults or unfreeze restrained bank accounts
Companies with long-standing judgments (5-20 years old) exploring enforcement options before expiration
diagnosis: Frank, Frank, Goldstein & Nager is best for New York-based creditors needing specialized debt collection litigation with flexible fees, or New York debtors defending against collection actions in court. The critical caveat: this is a debt collection firm first; while they legitimately defend debtors, their core business model prioritizes creditor recovery, and website security issues raise questions about operational standards that consumers should investigate before retention.
services: Debt collection demand letters and negotiation
Domestication of foreign judgments to New York
Debt defense and debt collection litigation defense
Bank account unfreezing and asset restraint release
Collections outsourcing and in-house collection consulting
